name: A re-established northbound :802 session RESUMES the TLS session (TCP-46)
hypothesis: When a northbound mbaps client re-establishes a dropped session, the gateway should offer TLS session resumption (RFC 5077 tickets / session IDs) so the reconnect skips a full ECDHE handshake — cheaper for a fleet of aggregators reconnecting after a WAN blip, and the mbtls client caches and offers the prior session. Whether the gateway ADVERTISES resumption is a server capability under test.
expected: The dropped session re-establishes (recovery) AND the handshake facts report Resumed=true — the gateway offered TLS session resumption so the reconnect skipped a full handshake. Observed live on the CC93 bench 2026-07-19: RESUMED over TLSv1.3/TLS13-AES128-GCM-SHA256.
fix: If Resumed=false (full handshake), the gateway stopped issuing/accepting session tickets on :802: check lexa-platform/wolfssl server context (session-cache / ticket support). If the reconnect itself fails, the session reaper is not freeing the dropped slot (max_sessions starvation).
notes: G1 (TLS-transport gap). Also subsumes the recovery invariant: the resume + follow-up discover succeed after a drop, so a re-established session both RECOVERS and RESUMES. Northbound (drives the aggregator's own :802 session via disconnect+resume), so it tests the gateway server, not a southbound sim.
